title: Depth Reduction R
text: Home * Search * Depth Reduction R R is a common name for a constant or variable signifying depth reduction used in the Null Move Pruning [1] , Null Move Reductions and independently in Multi-Cut [2] . In many null move implementations it is either 2 or 3, the exact formula being depth - R - 1 . Contents 1 Variable Reduction 2 Forum Posts 2.1 1997 ... 2.2 2000 ... 2.3 2010 ... 3 References Variable Reduction [ edit ] When R becomes a variable, then we say that a program uses adaptive null move pruning , first described by Ernst A. Heinz [3] , using R=3 when normal search depth exceeds 6 plies and R=2 otherwise, while more recently most engines account increased search depth with greater reductions [4] .
